user_query:
I’m organizing next month’s health and safety drills for our staff. Please use the files under input/ to plan April 2025 fire drills and prepare communications.

Inputs to use:
- input/context.json: contains a reference_date and drill_type. Treat all date calculations relative to reference_date.
- input/staff.csv: roster with each employee’s email and assigned shift.
- input/drill_log.csv: prior drills with participant emails; Participants are semicolon-separated.
- input/shifts.yaml: default start time and location per shift.

Requirements:
1) Determine the target “next month” and “last month” relative to reference_date in input/context.json. With reference_date 2025-03-15 and drill_type "fire", next month is April 2025 and last month is February 2025.
2) For each shift present in staff.csv (e.g., Day, Evening, Night), compute the "missed" set for last month as: all staff in that shift whose email does NOT appear in any drill_log.csv row where DrillType == drill_type and Shift == that shift and Date falls within the last calendar month. Use only last month’s rows for this calculation.
3) Create a schedule CSV at output/drill_plan_2025-04_fire.csv with exactly one row per shift. Columns:
   - Shift
   - DrillType (use value from context.json)
   - Date (a weekday between 2025-04-01 and 2025-04-30, inclusive)
   - StartTime (use default_time for that shift from input/shifts.yaml)
   - DurationMinutes (use value from context.json)
   - Location (use location for that shift from input/shifts.yaml)
   - RequiredAttendees (semicolon-separated emails of the "missed" set for that shift; this must exactly match what you computed in step 2)
   You may choose any valid weekday date within April 2025 for each shift; all other fields must come from the inputs.
4) Write a status summary report at output/drill_status_summary_2025-04_fire.md that, for each shift, shows:
   - Total staff in that shift (from staff.csv)
   - Count attended last month (from drill_log.csv, filtered as in step 2)
   - Count missed last month
   - The list of missed attendee emails (must match the RequiredAttendees you put in the schedule)
   Include a short note stating that the April drills are scheduled using the times/locations from shifts.yaml and will last DurationMinutes from context.json.
5) Draft one email per shift to notify those who missed last month. For each shift, create output/emails/<Shift>_fire_drill_2025-04.txt with:
   - A first line: To: <semicolon-separated list of the RequiredAttendees emails for that shift>
   - A second line: Subject: April 2025 Fire Drill - Attendance Required (Shift: <Shift>)
   - Body text on subsequent lines briefly stating the scheduled Date, StartTime, Location, DurationMinutes, and that attendance is mandatory for those listed.

Deliverables to produce:
- output/drill_plan_2025-04_fire.csv
- output/drill_status_summary_2025-04_fire.md
- output/emails/Day_fire_drill_2025-04.txt (and similarly for each shift present in staff.csv)

All outputs must be internally consistent with the input files and the rules above. Do not use any external data. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
